text.title: Flaky Test Elimination Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Flaky Test Elimination methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Root cause MUST be classified into one of: timing, ordering, state-leak, env-leak, non-determinism, infra, real-bug. 'Misc' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without classification, fixes are guesses; bucket maps to fix family.

rule.statement: Verification runs the test in isolation ≥50 times consecutively; pass count is recorded. Single-run 'now it works' is rejected.
rule.rationale: 1-in-50 flake rates are invisible at single-run scale.

rule.statement: Fix MUST address the classified root cause. 'Add a retry' or 'increase timeout' is rejected unless the root cause is genuinely 'real flake in dep we cannot fix'.
rule.rationale: Retries mask flake metrics and turn slow tests slower.

rule.statement: Fix ships with a test (or assertion) that fails when the regression returns. Implicit-only fixes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Flake fixes regress more often than feature fixes; explicit regression is the only defence.

rule.statement: Report attaches CI evidence of the 50-run pass count post-fix. Self-attested 'it passes now' is rejected.
rule.rationale: verification without evidence is the same as no verification.

rule.statement: Flake count is tracked weekly; the eliminated test is removed from the rolling tally.
rule.rationale: Measurement keeps the tail under control.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Flaky Test Elimination under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
